div.head: The Minister in Honduras (Morales) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
51. I have again requested the President and four candidates to meet on the [30th?] instant for the purpose of arriving at a definite agreement to maintain peace. All have signified their willingness
to attend the conference. It is probable that something may be accomplished. Bonilla informed me that he has no candidate to propose and will accept any honorable man as a compromise. Arias of the same opinion. Carias is doubtful and was led to believe, through malicious misrepresentations, that the Government of the United States desired his withdrawal. I quickly dispelled this misapprehension.
The President, Bonilla and Arias have requested my presence at the conference. I do not consider this advisable for obvious reasons, unless the Department desires me to attend.
